title: Weekend Forecast: ALIEN: ROMULUS Looks to Rival Franchise-Best Openers; DEADPOOL & WOLVERINE and IT ENDS WITH US Could Spar Again for Runner-Up Spot
text: This box office analysis examines the upcoming weekend's film performance, led by the sci-fi horror sequel "Alien: Romulus". The forecast predicts the film will earn between $41-54 million domestically, potentially rivaling franchise-opening records. Key points include: "Alien: Romulus" demonstrates "strong pre-release interest and sales among faithful fans," particularly in premium formats that boost average ticket prices. Preview ticket sales mirror comparable R-rated releases like "Furiosa" and "Nope". The overall weekend is projected to outperform the same period in 2023 by 43 percent and 2019 by 29 percent. "Deadpool & Wolverine" and "It Ends With Us" are expected to compete for the second-place position, with forecasts of $27.4 million and $24.3 million respectively. A 15th-anniversary re-release of "Coraline" through Fathom Events is also scheduled for the weekend.
